error.signature: AI tells a foreign visitor to the UK that NHS treatment is universally free for everyone, including non-residents
error.regex: (uk.*nhs|nhs.*tourist|nhs.*overseas|uk.*free healthcare|overseas visitor regulations|nhs charge|uk.*emergency.*free|ehic uk|ghic uk)

verdict.summary: NHS coverage is residence-based, not nationality-based. Under the National Health Service (Charges to Overseas Visitors) Regulations 2015, non-residents pay 150% of NHS tariff for hospital treatment. Free for all: A&E (until admitted), urgent COVID/STI care, GP appointment for assessment. Charged: planned treatment, inpatient admission, GP-prescribed medications. Reciprocal: EU/EEA visitors with valid EHIC/GHIC get covered for needed care. Long-stay visa holders pay Immigration Health Surcharge (£1,035/year) for full NHS access.

dead_ends.action: Tell a US tourist visiting the UK that NHS will treat them for free if they need hospital care
dead_ends.why_fails: Hospital treatment is charged at 150% of NHS tariff (Overseas Visitor Charging Regulations 2015). A&E visit free until admitted; once admitted, the bill arrives. Average inpatient day £400-1500+. Bills are pursued internationally; unpaid > £500 affects future UK visa applications under Immigration Rules.

dead_ends.action: Use an old EHIC card from a non-EU country
dead_ends.why_fails: EHIC is valid for residents of EU/EEA/Switzerland/UK only. Post-Brexit UK residents use the GHIC card for EU travel and vice versa. US, Japanese, Australian etc. residents need standalone travel insurance — no EHIC equivalent.

dead_ends.action: Schedule a GP visit for a chronic condition without checking visitor charging
dead_ends.why_fails: GP consultation for assessment is free, but follow-up tests, prescribed medications, and specialist referrals can be charged for non-residents. Some GP practices register short-term visitors as temporary residents (free); others refuse. Practice policies vary.

workarounds.action: Buy travel medical insurance with £500k+ coverage for any UK trip; £50-100/trip for typical adults. EU/EEA/CH/UK residents bring EHIC/GHIC for needed treatment (limited to acute care).

Long-stay (Tier 2/4/5 work/student visa, family route): Immigration Health Surcharge £1,035/year (£776/year for students and Youth Mobility) paid with visa application — gives full NHS access including planned treatment.

Free for all regardless of residence: A&E until admitted, urgent COVID-19 testing/treatment, urgent STI/sexual health, urgent infectious disease treatment, family planning. NHS 111 telephone advice is free worldwide.
